headline: MGM Resorts (MGM) Stock May Be 23% Undervalued After Buyout Probe
summary: MGM Resorts International stock has gained 28.5% year to date, yet the valuation picture is split, with a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) intrinsic value estimate pointing to meaningful upside while market multiples suggest the shares are not cheap. Year to date, MGM Resorts International is up 28.5%, so the stock is already pricing in a good deal of optimism. The proposed US$48.30 per share buyout offer and the ongoing expansion of BetMGM and global resorts can support longer term cash flow...

headline: MGM Resorts International (MGM) After Barry Diller Bid Raises A Fresh Valuation Question
summary: MGM Resorts International (MGM) is back in focus after Barry Diller’s People Inc. publicly proposed acquiring the remaining shares at $48.30 each, valuing the casino and entertainment group at around $12.4 billion. See our latest analysis for MGM Resorts International. MGM Resorts International’s share price has eased over the past month with a 30 day share price return of down 4.27%, but the 90 day share price return of 27.56% and 1 year total shareholder return of 23.60% point to momentum...
